spec_match.rationale: Reads Animation.overallProgress for a real Web Animation with configurable iterations/direction/delay, matching the spec formula, but does not surface the spec edge cases (null when no timeline, 0 with infinite endTime, behaviour before the delay window).

interactive.rationale: Iterations, direction, and delay inputs plus play/stop controls drive a live animation and emit live currentTime/manual/overallProgress gauges.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb promises a 0..1 progress value that collapses iterations/direction bookkeeping; the demo delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: No coverage of the spec's defined null result (no associated effect / unresolved current time), infinite effect end (returns 0), or sub-delay sampling. Only the happy path is exercised.

references.rationale: The see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5083257285378048 plus the canonical drafts.csswg.org spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from CSS variables, no static-card pattern, no raw hex values, and the global button styles are inherited untouched.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the null overallProgress result
openQuestions.detail: The spec explicitly defines overallProgress as null when the animation has no associated effect or no resolved current time. Add a button that creates such an animation (e.g. anim.cancel() then read .overallProgress) and shows the null readout — without that, the reader can't tell what the property does when an animation is not playing.

openQuestions.title: Add an infinite-iteration case
openQuestions.detail: Per spec, an animation with iterations: Infinity yields effect end = Infinity, so overallProgress is 0 forever. The current iteration input is capped at 10 — add an `Infinity` toggle so authors see that the property degrades predictably and is not a runtime progress reading for endless animations.

openQuestions.title: Show pre-delay vs post-end clamping
openQuestions.detail: Scrubbing currentTime before the delay window and past the end of the effect both clamp to 0 and 1 respectively; that behaviour is currently invisible because scrub.max is set to the computed endTime. Allow scrubbing past the bounds (or stepping through with delay > 0) to show the clamp.

summary: Solid baseline that exercises iterations/direction/delay through a real Web Animation and contrasts overallProgress with the manual formula. Underweights spec edge cases (null result, infinite end, pre-delay/post-end clamping) — adding those would move it from competent to definitive.
